authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(119) — Rock Creek Diversion Channel (Mono Co.)
sources.quotedText: (119) Rock Creek Diversion Channel (Mono Co.) from its source below Tom's Place to its confluence with Crooked Creek.  Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
Frequently Asked Fishing Questions | Visit Mono County
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Populated Place layer record for gaz_id 1660015, "Toms Place", Mono County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":1660015,"gaz_name":"Toms Place","gaz_featureclass":"Populated Place","fcode":61500,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mono"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13211536.2052, 4517639.9102) converts to lon -118.68125, lat 37.56133. GNIS has no distinct 'Rock Creek Diversion Channel' feature record (checked MapServer layers 6 and 7 by name and by LIKE search), so this Toms Place point — matching CCR 7.50(b)(119)'s own description of the channel's source as 'below Tom's Place', and corroborated by the adjacent GNIS 'Rock Creek' stream point (gaz_id 234580) at -118.68014, 37.56105 — is used as the anchor. The file's prior coordinate (-118.6720, 37.5680) was ~1.1km from this anchor and has been corrected.

parameters.note: Rock Creek Diversion Channel from its source below Tom's Place to its confluence with Crooked Creek is closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, from its source below Tom's Place to its confluence with Crooked Creek
regulations.verbatimText: from its source below Tom's Place to its confluence with Crooked Creek.  Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. This water is closed to all fishing all year, so the license requirement is moot here, but is included for statewide-standard completeness.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
